# llms.txt — Aigency Router v2

## Project Summary

Aigency Router v2 is a multi-agent AI skill orchestration hub that distributes 53+ BMad skills to 18+ AI coding agent platforms via symbolic links.

## Quick Facts

- **Type**: Content and configuration repository (no build, no server)
- **Primary Language**: Markdown (SKILL.md files)
- **Framework**: BMad (Business Modeler & Developer)
- **Skills**: 53 BMad + 3 external (Stripe)
- **Agent Platforms**: 18+ (Claude, Cline, Goose, Qwen, Roo, Trae, etc.)
- **Agent Personas**: 7 (Analyst, PM, UX, Architect, Dev, Tech Writer, Test Architect)
- **Modules**: BMM (planning), TEA (testing), Core (framework)
- **Memory**: ICM (Intelligent Context Memory) — mandatory

## Key Directories

- `.qwen/skills/` — Canonical BMad skill store (53 skills)
- `.agents/skills/` — Shared external skills (Stripe best-practices)
- `_bmad/` — Framework configuration and scripts
- `docs/` — Project documentation and orchestration plans

## Important Files

- `_bmad/config.toml` — Resolved configuration (read-only, installer-managed)
- `_bmad/_config/skill-manifest.csv` — Skill directory index
- `skills-lock.json` — SHA-256 hashes for external skill integrity
- `AGENTS.md` — Agent context and conventions (ICM mandatory)

## Architecture

Hub-and-spoke distribution: canonical skill stores → symlink → agent directories. Configuration merges via layered TOML: core → modules → custom team → custom user.

## Conventions

- Skills are directories with `SKILL.md` containing frontmatter (name, description, triggers)
- Never edit `_bmad/config.toml` directly — use `_bmad/custom/` overrides
- Always run `python _bmad/scripts/resolve_config.py` after manifest changes
- ICM stores are mandatory at 5 trigger points (errors, decisions, preferences, tasks, 20+ calls)
